title: 11 Effective Herbal Remedies for Migraine Sufferers

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anethole is good for migraine because it has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that help reduce swelling and ease migraine headaches.
Polyphenols are good for migraine because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling in the blood vessels of the brain.

Flavonoids are good for migraine because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation in the blood vessels of the brain, which are often responsible for migraine headaches.
Foeniculin is good for migraine because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce the swelling and pain in blood vessels in the brain, which are often the cause of migraine headaches.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, has been used for a long time to help with migraine. The tincture made from its seeds is rich in compounds that can ease migraine symptoms. One of these compounds is anethole, which has been shown to reduce inflammation in the body. This is important because inflammation is often a cause of migraines. When inflammation decreases, the pain and discomfort associated with migraines may also decrease. Additionally, anethole has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that can trigger migraines. Fennel tincture may also help to relax muscles in the neck and scalp, which can be a source of migraine pain. By reducing inflammation, calming the nervous system, and relaxing muscles, fennel tincture may provide relief from migraine symptoms. Fennel's compounds may also help to stabilize blood flow to the brain, which can be disrupted during a migraine.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Boswellia serrata herbal tincture that helps with migraines, you will need dried Boswellia serrata bark or resin, a glass jar, a clean glass dropper, and a solvent like vodka or glycerin. Start by measuring one part of the dried Boswellia serrata herb to two parts of the solvent in the glass jar, making sure the herb is completely covered. Close the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the herb extract into the solvent. This process is called steeping or maceration, and it allows the herbal properties to infuse into the solvent. After the steeping period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar, discarding the solids. Your Boswellia serrata tincture is now ready to use, and you can take it as needed to help alleviate migraine symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal tinctures for migraine

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Valerenic acid is good for migraine because it helps to reduce inflammation and relax tense muscles in the head and neck, which are common causes of migraine pain.
Valeranone is good for migraine because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation in the brain, which are common causes of migraine headaches.
Valerenal is good for migraine because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce pain and inflammation in the brain, which is a major cause of migraine headaches.

Isovaleric acid is good for migraine because it helps to reduce inflammation in the brain, which can cause migraine pain, by blocking the production of inflammatory chemicals and promoting relaxation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis tincture, also known as valerian root tincture, is often used to help with migraine symptoms. The root of the valerian plant contains compounds that help the body relax and reduce stress. When we're stressed, our blood vessels constrict, which can lead to migraine headaches. Valerian root tincture helps to calm the nervous system, making our blood vessels relax and open up. This can help reduce the severity of migraine headaches. Valerian root tincture also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and swelling associated with migraines. Some people find that taking valerian root tincture before bedtime helps them sleep better, which is also a key factor in reducing migraine frequency and severity. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress, valerian root tincture may help alleviate migraine symptoms and provide relief for those who suffer from them.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Boswellia serrata herbal tincture, start by gathering the herb, which is also known as frankincense. You can buy it in dried or fresh form from a health food store or online. If using dried Boswellia, crush it into smaller pieces to release its active properties. Next, combine the crushed herb with a solvent like vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar, making sure the herb is completely covered. The general ratio is 1 part herb to 2 parts solvent. Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for two to six weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the solvent extract the herb's properties. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. The resulting tincture can be taken sublingually (under the tongue) or added to water to help alleviate migraine symptoms, which may include inflammation and pain relief.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal tinctures for migraine

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol is good for migraine because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to migraine headaches.
Shogaol is good for migraine because it reduces inflammation and blocks pain-causing chemicals in the body, leading to fewer and less severe migraine attacks.

Polyphenols are good for migraine because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation in the blood vessels of the brain, which are often involved in migraine attacks.
Zingiberene is good for migraine because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and pain in the blood vessels of the brain, which can cause migraine headaches.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, is a popular natural remedy for migraines. The tincture form of ginger is easy to take and can provide quick relief from migraine symptoms. Ginger contains compounds called gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and ease the pain in the head and neck. When taken before or during a migraine, ginger tincture can help to relax the muscles, improve blood flow, and reduce nausea and vomiting. Ginger's natural anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce the severity and frequency of migraines by preventing blood vessel spasms and inflammation. Additionally, ginger has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety, common migraine triggers.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Boswellia serrata herbal tincture that can help with migraines, you'll first need to gather the necessary ingredients and equipment. Start by drying and grinding 2-3 ounces of Boswellia serrata roots or bark into a fine powder. Then, mix 1 part of the powdered Boswellia with 2 parts of a solvent like vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar. The solvent helps to extract the active compounds from the Boswellia. Next, store the mixture in a cool, dark place and let it steep for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to help the ingredients mix. After the steeping period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, and discard the solids. Now, your Boswellia serrata tincture is ready to use. To take it, mix 20-30 drops of the tincture with water or another liquid, and consume it up to 3 times a day as needed to help alleviate migraine symptoms. Always consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal remedies, especially if you're already taking medications or have underlying health conditions.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal tinctures for migraine

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for migraine because it helps reduce inflammation and pain by blocking the production of certain chemicals in the body that cause these symptoms.

Ginkgolide B in Boswellia Serrata is good for migraine because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help block the activity of certain chemicals that cause pain and inflammation in the brain, leading to migraine relief.
Isorhapontigenin is good for migraine because it helps reduce inflammation in the blood vessels of the brain, which is a common cause of migraine headaches.
Bilobalide is good for migraine because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation in the brain and blood vessels, which can contribute to migraine attacks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tincture is often used to help with migraines because of its properties that improve blood flow to the brain. When a migraine happens, blood vessels in the brain get wider and cause pain. Ginkgo biloba helps to stop this from happening by relaxing these blood vessels, reducing their sensitivity. This helps to ease the pain and discomfort associated with migraines. The tincture may also help to reduce inflammation, which is another cause of migraine pain. Some people also find that Ginkgo biloba helps to improve their mood, reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to migraines. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba has antioxidant properties that protect the brain from damage and help to reduce the frequency and severity of migraines.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Boswellia serrata herbal tincture that helps with migraine, you will need Boswellia serrata roots, a clean glass jar with a lid, a coffee filter, vodka or a high-proof alcohol, and a dark glass bottle with a dropper. First, dry the Boswellia serrata roots in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying them for a few days. Then, chop the dried roots into small pieces and fill the glass jar about 3/4 full with the chopped roots. Pour enough vodka over the roots to completely cover them, making sure the roots are fully submerged in the liquid. Close the jar with the lid and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar every day. After the infusion time has passed, strain the liquid through a coffee filter into the dark glass bottle, discarding the solids. This tincture can be taken sublingually (under the tongue) as needed to help alleviate migraine symptoms. Start with a small dose, such as 10-20 drops, and gradually increase the amount as needed.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal tinctures for migraine

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for migraine because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation in the brain, making it easier to manage migraine symptoms.
Flavonoids are good for migraine because they help reduce inflammation and ease pain by blocking the production of chemical messengers that cause headaches.

Kaempferol is good for migraine because it helps to reduce inflammation and relax blood vessels, which can help to ease migraine pain and frequency by blocking the release of chemicals that cause blood vessels to swell.

Tryptophan is good for migraine because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce the pain and swelling of blood vessels in the brain, which are often the main causes of a migraine attack.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata tincture, also known as passionflower, has been used for centuries to help with migraine symptoms. The reason it's effective is due to its ability to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety. When we experience a migraine, our brain and nerves can get overactive, leading to pain and discomfort. Passionflower tincture contains compounds like flavonoids and alkaloids that help to slow down brain activity, making it easier to relax and reduce the frequency and severity of migraine attacks. It can also help to reduce inflammation and relax tense muscles, which are common causes of migraine pain. Some people find that taking passionflower tincture before bed helps to prevent migraines during the night, while others use it to alleviate symptoms during an attack. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using passionflower tincture, especially if you're already taking medication or have other health conditions.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Boswellia serrata herbal tincture for migraine relief, you'll need dried Boswellia serrata resin, a clean glass jar with a lid, a clean glass dropper, and a neutral liquid like vodka or glycerin. First, prepare your workspace by washing your hands and cleaning your equipment thoroughly. Then, measure about 1 cup of dried Boswellia serrata resin and place it in the glass jar. Next, add about 2 cups of your chosen neutral liquid to the jar, making sure the resin is completely covered. Stir the mixture well and seal the jar with its lid. Now, store the jar in a cool, dark place for about 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to allow the resin to infuse into the liquid. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Your Boswellia serrata tincture is now ready to use. To take it, simply place a few drops of the tincture under your tongue, where it can be absorbed quickly into your bloodstream, helping to alleviate migraine symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, commonly known as English lavender, has been used for centuries to help with headaches and migraines. The tincture made from this plant contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate that have a calming effect on the body. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Boswellia serrata herbal tincture that helps with migraine, you will need Boswellia serrata resin, high-proof vodka or ethanol, and a clean glass jar. First, dry the Boswellia serrata resin under the sun or using a food dehydrator to remove excess moisture. Once dry, chop the resin into small pieces and fill a clean glass jar about 2/3 full. Next, pour enough high-proof vodka or ethanol over the resin to cover it completely. Close the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for about 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. This process allows the resin to infuse into the liquid, creating a strong medicinal tincture. After the infusion period, strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your Boswellia serrata tincture is now ready to use, typically taken in small amounts (about 20-30 drops) under the tongue, 2-3 times a day, to help alleviate migraine symptoms.

Avena sativa also contains compounds that may help to reduce inflammation and improve the body's response to stress. Some people find that taking Avena sativa tincture helps to alleviate migraine symptoms such as pain, sensitivity to light and sound, and nausea. It's thought to work by promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety, and improving mood, which can all contribute to a reduction in migraine frequency and severity. Avena sativa is often used in combination with other natural remedies, such as feverfew and butterbur, to help manage migraine symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Boswellia serrata herbal tincture that helps with migraine, you will need dried Boswellia serrata roots, vodka, and a glass jar with a lid. First, combine one part of dried Boswellia serrata roots with two parts of vodka in the glass jar, making sure the roots are completely covered by the vodka. The general ratio is 1:2 or 1:3, depending on the desired strength of the tincture. Next, seal the jar with its lid and store it in a cool, dark place for about two to three weeks. Shake the jar every day to help the vodka extract the active compounds from the roots. remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, is a natural remedy for migraine headaches. The rosmarinic acid in Melissa officinalis tincture also has a vasodilating effect, which means it helps to widen the blood vessels in the brain and improve blood flow. This can help to reduce the severity of migraine headaches and prevent them from occurring in the first place. Additionally, Melissa officinalis has a sedative effect, which can help to promote relaxation and reduce stress, both of which are common triggers for migraines. remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus, also known as chaste tree, is a plant that's been used for centuries to help with hormone balance and other health issues. You will also need a clean glass jar with a lid, a cutting tool, and a solvent like vodka or glycerin. remedies.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata tincture is a natural remedy that may help with migraines. The main reason is that it has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling in the blood vessels of the brain. This swelling is often what causes the severe headache pain associated with migraines. By reducing inflammation, the Boswellia serrata tincture may help alleviate migraine symptoms. Additionally, it can help relax the muscles and calm the nervous system, which can also contribute to migraine relief. Some people find that it can help reduce the frequency and severity of migraines. It's thought that the active compounds in Boswellia serrata, called boswellic acids, work together to block chemicals in the body that can trigger migraine pain. This natural remedy is often taken as a tincture, which is a liquid extract of the plant.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Boswellia serrata herbal tinctures for migraine relief, start by gathering 1 part of dried Boswellia serrata roots and 2 parts of vodka or a high-proof spirit. Combine the dried roots and spirit in a clean glass jar, making sure the roots are completely covered by the liquid. Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the spirit extract the medicinal properties from the roots. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Add a small amount of glycerin to the liquid to help preserve it and enhance its shelf life. Transfer the tincture to a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid, and store it in a cool, dark place. intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can be a helpful natural remedy for people who experience migraines. Migraines are severe headaches that can be debilitating and disrupt daily life. Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, is a herb that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity of migraine symptoms. It also has a compound called anethole, which can help relax muscles and improve digestion, both of which can contribute to migraines. Valeriana officinalis, or valerian root, is another herb that can help alleviate migraine symptoms. It contains compounds that can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for migraines. By using herbal tinctures made from these herbs, people can potentially reduce the frequency and severity of their migraines. This can improve their quality of life by allowing them to participate in activities they enjoy and maintain their daily routines without being held back by debilitating headaches.
supplementary_best: The best combination of herbal tinctures for migraine relief can be achieved by combining Zingiber officinale, Foeniculum vulgare, and Passiflora incarnata. Zingiber officinale helps to reduce inflammation and relax muscles, which is helpful in relieving migraine pain and tension. Foeniculum vulgare has a compound called anethole, which acts as a natural pain reliever and has anti-inflammatory properties. Passiflora incarnata, also known as passionflower, is a natural sedative that can help to reduce anxiety and stress, which are often triggers for migraines.  This combination is effective because it addresses multiple aspects of migraine relief. Zingiber officinale and Foeniculum vulgare provide physical relief from pain and inflammation, while Passiflora incarnata addresses the emotional and mental triggers of migraines. By combining these three herbal tinctures, you can get comprehensive relief from migraine symptoms.  The dose of this combination can be 10-20 drops of each tincture taken together 2-3 times a day as needed.
supplementary_avoid: If you have migraines, it's best to avoid tinctures made from certain herbs that can trigger or worsen symptoms. One such herb is 'Cinchona officinalaline' from Cinchona, which contains quinine, a known migraine trigger. Quinine can cause blood vessels to constrict, leading to migraine symptoms.  Another herb to avoid is 'Glycyrrhiza glabra', or licorice root. High doses of glycyrrhizin, a compound found in licorice root, can cause blood vessels to constrict, leading to headaches and migraines.  Additionally, 'Paeonia lactiflora', or Chinese peony, may not be suitable for those with migraines due to its potential to interact with blood thinners and worsen migraine symptoms.  Lastly, 'Polygonum multiflorum', or he shou wu, may also be problematic for individuals with migraines. This herb can increase the risk of bleeding, which can exacerbate migraine symptoms.  It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if you have a pre-existing condition like migraines.
supplementary_other_remedies: Magnesium can help ease migraine pain. This mineral is found in many foods like dark leafy greens, nuts, and seeds. When we don't get enough magnesium, our blood vessels constrict, which can lead to migraine headaches. Taking magnesium supplements or applying magnesium creams to the skin can help relax blood vessels and reduce pain. Some people also use Epsom salt baths to soak up magnesium.  Feverfew is an herb, but it can be taken in a different form - as a supplement. It contains compounds that can help stop blood vessels from expanding and cause pain. Feverfew also has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and ease migraine symptoms.  Aromatherapy can be helpful for migraines too. Essential oils like lavender and peppermint can be inhaled to calm the mind and body. When we feel stressed, our muscles tense up, which can trigger a migraine. Inhaling these oils can help us relax and reduce muscle tension.  Cold or warm compresses can be applied to the neck and shoulders to ease tension and pain. When we're tense, our muscles can become inflamed, which can lead to pain. Applying a cold or warm compress can help reduce inflammation and relax the muscles.  Acupuncture is another natural remedy that has been shown to help with migraines. This involves inserting thin needles into specific points on the body to stimulate healing and relaxation. Some people believe that acupuncture can help balance the body's energy, which can help ease migraine symptoms.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with migraines often experience other health problems that can be helped by medicinal herbs. Many of these herbs have anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce pain and swelling. Feverfew, for example, has been shown to reduce migraine frequency and severity by preventing blood vessels from expanding and causing pain. Ginger has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help ease nausea and vomiting, which are common symptoms of migraines. Butterbur, another herb, has been found to reduce migraine frequency by blocking the release of substances that can cause pain and inflammation.  Some people with migraines also experience anxiety and depression, which can be treated with herbs like passionflower and St. John's Wort. Passionflower has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation. St. John's Wort has been used for centuries to treat mild depression and can help improve mood and reduce symptoms of anxiety.  Magnesium deficiency is common in people with migraines, and herbs like magnesium-rich ashwagandha and ginkgo biloba can help alleviate symptoms. Ashwagandha has anti-inflammatory properties and can help reduce stress and anxiety, while ginkgo biloba can improve blood flow and reduce migraine frequency.  Other herbs, like willow bark and devil's claw, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and inflammation. Willow bark contains salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which can help reduce pain and inflammation. Devil's claw has been used for centuries to treat pain and inflammation and can help reduce migraine frequency.  Herbs like lavender and peppermint can also help alleviate migraine symptoms. Lavender has a calming effect on the nervous system and can promote relaxation, while peppermint can help ease nausea and vomiting. These herbs can be consumed as tea, taken as supplements, or applied topically to relieve pain and inflammation.
